slides.notes: Open on the promise. Beacon detects wildfire ignitions in under 3 minutes.

props.title: See the first ember.
props.sub: Beacon fuses satellite thermal imagery with a mesh of edge sensors to confirm a new ignition in under three minutes — while it's still a campfire, not a catastrophe.

slides.notes: The pain + the dollar figure. Detection today is too slow.

props.title: By the time a wildfire is reported, it has already won.
points: The average ignition burns undetected for 45+ minutes before a 911 call.
Once a fire crowns, every minute of delay multiplies the acres lost.
Lookout towers and citizen reports miss the remote ignitions that grow worst.

figure.label: in annual U.S. wildfire losses — property, suppression, health, and lost output

slides.notes: What Beacon does + the headline result: sub-3-minute detection.

props.title: A detection network that never blinks.
points: Geostationary thermal feeds scan the wildland every 30 seconds.
Solar edge sensors corroborate heat, smoke, and gas at ground level.
A confirmed ignition with GPS coordinates lands on the dispatcher's screen.

figure.label: median time from ignition to a verified, located alert

slides.notes: Four steps: sense, detect, verify, alert.

props.title: From the first photon of heat to boots on the ground.

steps.title: Sense
steps.desc: Satellite thermal bands and solar-powered edge nodes watch every acre, day and night.

steps.title: Detect
steps.desc: On-device models flag a thermal anomaly the instant it breaks from the landscape's baseline.

steps.title: Verify
steps.desc: Cross-sensor fusion rejects flares, glint, and equipment heat — driving false alarms toward zero.

steps.title: Alert
steps.desc: A geolocated ignition alert routes to agencies and crews through the channels they already use.

slides.notes: TAM / SAM / SOM as stacked survey bars.

props.title: Climate has made early detection a line item, not a luxury.

segments.label: TAM
segments.note: Global wildfire detection, monitoring & resilience spend by 2030

segments.label: SAM
segments.note: Utilities, insurers & land agencies in fire-prone regions

segments.label: SOM
segments.note: Western U.S. utilities & state agencies, 3-year reachable

props.title: Every deployment makes the next ignition easier to catch.

slides.notes: Founding team with wildfire, space, and ML pedigree.

props.title: Built by people who've fought fire and flown the sensors.

slides.notes: The ask + use of funds: scale the sensor mesh.

props.title: Series A to blanket the American West before the next fire season.
